# Bootstrap admin, register a second identity, and exercise the Tier-1/Tier-2 RBAC
# split end-to-end against a real daemon (auto-started by the first command).
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

# A fresh name needs no auth — this IS the bootstrap path, and the first-ever
# identity auto-gets the admin role.
exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token

exec breeze identity register alice
cp stdout alice.token

# Tier-1: whoami/ps need no token at all.
exec breeze whoami --as admin
stdout admin

exec breeze ps --json
stdout '"name":\s*"alice"'

# Tier-2: role assign requires the admin's own token.
exec breeze role assign reviewer alice --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze role list --json
stdout '"name":\s*"alice"[\s\S]*"reviewer"'

# A non-admin can't assign roles.
! exec breeze role assign reviewer alice --as alice --token-file alice.token
stderr 'requires admin'

exec breeze stop
